task_completion.description: Agent logged into the FreeMED application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeMED dashboard or main navigation interface after a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the Lab Tests dictionary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Lab Tests' module, dictionary, or list view within the FreeMED interface.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the form to add a new lab test
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an empty or partially filled form for creating a new Lab Test entry.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct Test Name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Helicobacter pylori Breath Test' is visible inside the Test Name input field on the form.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct Test Code/Abbreviation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'UBT-HP' is visible inside the Test Code or Abbreviation input field on the form.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the record and it is available in the dictionary
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a success message indicating the record was saved, or the new test 'Helicobacter pylori Breath Test' ('UBT-HP') is visible in the master list of Lab Tests.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with FreeMED
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeMED interface is shown inside a web browser window, not being manipulated via curl or API scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent typed inputs into the actual FreeMED web form
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected name and code are seen inside the HTML input fields of the FreeMED application, not just written in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing MySQL, SQLite, or phpMyAdmin interfaces being used to insert the record directly into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must add a lab test with the exact Test Name 'Helicobacter pylori Breath Test' and Test Code/Abbreviation 'UBT-HP'.
